meta.tagline: Scene round-up · gigs · jams · underground picks
meta.summary: Spring into summer: Paradiso and Bimhuis pack the month with touring acts, ADE 30th-anniversary planning is live, and the underground stack (Alternative, Radar Squat, Subbacultcha) remains the best route to lesser-known nights.

roundup.title: May 2026 — what's happening in Amsterdam
paragraphs: May is the bridge month: terraces are full, festival season hasn't peaked yet, and the big rooms are stacking touring bills before summer park programming takes over. Paradiso and Melkweg both run dense calendars through the 31st — from post-punk legends and Belpop comebacks to double-night Goose runs and late-month COBRAH — while Bimhuis lines up a serious jazz fortnight with John Scofield, Delvon Lamarr, and Aaron Parks Little Big.

Below the headline tours, the city's everyday music life keeps moving: Tuesday jams at Bimhuis, rooftop and community-centre open mics, café sessions in the Jordaan and West, and bar stages around Leidseplein. If you're hunting nights that won't appear on I amsterdam's front page, stack Amsterdam Alternative, Radar Squat, Subbacultcha, and the Offbeat wiki — then cross-check venue newsletters from OCCII, OT301, Zaal 100, and Paradiso's smaller rooms.
Industry-side, ADE has confirmed 21–25 October 2026 for its 30th anniversary year. May is the right moment to watch Pro Pass updates and venue pre-announcements rather than waiting until autumn.

items.title: I amsterdam — concerts & gigs calendar
items.snippet: Official citywide listing for live music across Amsterdam — useful as a first pass before you drill into venue agendas or genre-specific feeds.

items.title: Weekend guide — hidden-gem picks
items.snippet: I amsterdam’s weekly digest surfaces smaller bills and one-off nights you might miss on big-venue homepages alone.

items.title: Bazart + NONCHELANGE — Paradiso Main Hall
items.snippet: Belgian Belpop trio Bazart returns to Paradiso after a three-year break with new album material; NONCHELANGE opens.

items.title: Joey Valence & Brae — HYPERYOUTH at Paradiso
items.snippet: High-energy punk-rap duo with Kelderbar afterparty (L Dopa) — club night bundled with the main hall show.

items.title: Sofia Isella — two nights at Melkweg
items.snippet: Back-to-back pop headline dates with rotating supports — rare double bill for a rising international act.

items.title: Les Négresses Vertes — Paradiso Main Hall
items.snippet: French punk-chanson icons return on the Zobi Tour — high-energy Main Hall comeback show.

items.title: Public Image Ltd. — Melkweg
items.snippet: Post-punk legends on the May calendar — one of the month's anchor rock bookings.

items.title: Goose (USA) — double header at Melkweg
items.snippet: Belgian electro-rock favourites play consecutive nights — strong week for guitar-led dance crossover.

items.title: COBRAH — Melkweg
items.snippet: Electro-pop closer for May — pair with Paradiso's Sonic Journey night the same day for a club/live split.

items.title: Bandsintown — touring alerts for Amsterdam
items.snippet: Follow venues or artists to get notified when tours add Amsterdam dates — handy alongside official venue calendars.

items.title: Spotify concerts — listen-led discovery
items.snippet: Spotify’s concerts view ties listening habits to nearby shows — good for catching artists you already stream.

items.title: Bimhuis — May programme
items.snippet: Waterfront jazz institution: May brings John Scofield & Gerald Clayton Duo (10th), Jazz Orchestra of the Concertgebouw (16–17), Fra Fra Sound, Delvon Lamarr Organ Trio (22nd), Aaron Parks Little Big (23rd), and more.

items.title: John Scofield & Gerald Clayton Duo
items.snippet: Flagship May booking — guitar and piano duo at the waterfront hall.

items.title: Delvon Lamarr Organ Trio — Bimhuis
items.snippet: Soul-jazz organ trio — one of the month's most danceable jazz rooms.

items.title: Bimhuis jam session
items.snippet: Weekly jazz jam on the Bimhuis stage — professional room for sit-ins; pair with our map pin for directions and contact.

items.title: Café de Engelbewaarder — jazz sessions
items.snippet: Historic Jordaan café with regular live jazz — intimate room, classic Amsterdam listening atmosphere.

items.title: Zaal 100 — Impro Jazzcafé
items.snippet: West Amsterdam impro jazz café — community players and fixed-session regulars; less commercial, very welcoming.

items.title: Amsterdam Sights — free jazz & low-cost sessions
items.snippet: Practical list of free or cheap jazz and open-air music — useful for budget nights alongside café sessions.

items.title: Bourbon Street — live sets & late jams
items.snippet: Leidseplein institution for frequent live music and late-night sets — blues, jazz, and jam-friendly programming. Separate venue from The Waterhole next door.

items.title: ADE 2026 — 30th anniversary dates confirmed
items.snippet: Amsterdam Dance Event returns 21–25 October for its 30th year — festival, conference (Pro + Lab), and 1,200+ events across 300+ venues.

items.title: Buma Cultuur — ADE platform overview
items.snippet: Industry context for ADE as Europe’s leading electronic music platform — conference tracks on tech, sustainability, and talent.

items.title: ADE Pro Pass & festival access
items.snippet: Pro Pass bundles conference, festival, and Arts & Culture programmes — early planning helps for October week.

items.title: Paradiso — Sonic Journey: house & techno
items.snippet: Paradiso programming spans club culture — May closes with a Sonic Journey night tracing house and techno roots.

items.title: Tolhuistuin — mixed-format nights
items.snippet: NDSM-side venue for club nights, live electronic hybrids, and cultural crossovers — check agenda for May listings.

items.title: OT301 — experimental electronic
items.snippet: OT301 programmes experimental and underground electronic work — strong for left-field nights outside the mainstream circuit.

items.title: Bex — first NL headline at Paradiso Upstairs
items.snippet: London-via-Belgium artist blends softrap, R&B and indie — Upstairs Room headline debut in Amsterdam.

items.title: Dorian Electra — Paradiso Upstairs
items.snippet: Theatrical pop provocateur on the Fanfare cycle — maximalist live show in the smaller Paradiso room.

items.title: Subbacultcha — emerging & alternative bills
items.snippet: Membership platform and agenda for indie, experimental, and alternative Amsterdam — strong for artists pre-mainstream breakout.

items.title: OCCII — punk, indie & DIY gigs
items.snippet: Long-running squat-culture venue on Amstelveenseweg — alternative gigs and community-led programming.

items.title: Bitterzoet — smaller live bills
items.snippet: Compact room on Spuistraat for multi-genre live shows — good bridge between café sessions and Paradiso-scale gigs.

items.title: De Nieuwe Anita — indie & variety nights
items.snippet: Frederiksplein venue for indie bills, film, and variety programming — community favourite for less commercial line-ups.

items.title: Pretty Pissed 2026 — Melkweg
items.snippet: Punk/indie showcase with Wap Shoo Wap afterparty — one of May's loudest community bills.

items.title: Antony Szmierek — Melkweg
items.snippet: Poetic indie/spoken-word crossover — late-month pick for literary live music fans.

items.title: Zoku — Rooftop Open Mic Night
items.snippet: Sign-up 17:30–18:00 for the rooftop open mic — hotel-creative space welcoming musicians; on our jam map.

items.title: Buurthuis de Sering — Open Mic Night
items.snippet: West Amsterdam community-centre open mic mixing music and performance — friendly neighbourhood stage.

items.title: Café de Pianist — jazz open sessions
items.snippet: Jazz café on Van der Helstplein with regular live sessions — sit-in friendly atmosphere.

items.title: The Waterhole — live gigs calendar
items.snippet: Leidseplein bar with frequent live sets and open-stage friendly programming — check gigs page for May slots.

items.title: Bourbon Street — live music & jams
items.snippet: Leidseplein club with nightly live sets — distinct from The Waterhole; blues, jazz, and late-night jam-friendly programming.

items.title: Eventbrite — Amsterdam open mic listings
items.snippet: Aggregator for small recurring open mics and community events — useful for one-off sign-up nights.

items.title: Joe's Garage — jam-friendly bar stage
items.snippet: Noord community bar known for informal live music and jam-friendly nights — listed in offbeat venue guides.

items.title: Amsterdam Alternative — counterculture agenda
items.snippet: Best single feed for free spaces, squats, and underground events — start here for non-commercial nights.

items.title: Radar Squat — DIY & benefit nights
items.snippet: Squat-scene calendar for niche gigs, benefits, and DIY venues — pairs with Alternative for full underground coverage.

items.title: Offbeat Amsterdam — community wiki
items.snippet: Reddit-curated offbeat events and lesser-known nights — community tips for Vondelbunker, Vrankrijk, Cinetol, and more.

items.title: Vrankrijk — anarchist social centre events
items.snippet: Spuistraat social centre hosting punk, political, and DIY nights — check Alternative/Radar for current listings.

items.title: Vondelbunker — park bunker programme
items.snippet: Seasonal underground programming in Vondelpark’s bunker — often low-key experimental and community-run.

items.title: Cinetol — intimate underground room
items.snippet: Small-scale gigs and niche nights — frequently cited in secret-gig stacks alongside Zaal 100 and OT301.

items.title: Secret-gig stack — how to combine sources
items.snippet: Highest hit-rate: Amsterdam Alternative + Radar Squat + Subbacultcha + Offbeat wiki, then venue newsletters (OCCII, OT301, Paradiso).
